title: format.ts non-finite guard is the live correctness defect (immigration-paperwork); status aggregation already hardened
content: In immigration-paperwork, the case-file status-aggregation modules (criteria.ts summarizeCriteria/statusTone, case-list.ts filter/sort/query) are ALREADY runtime-hardened and tested under ADR-0001/0002 — no live defect there; do not re-open. The remaining grounded correctness defect is src/lib/format.ts: helpers trust an unvalidated number — formatPercent (format.ts:30) THROWS TypeError on null/undefined via .toFixed (crash) and emits NaN%/$NaN on non-finite. Settled decision (ADR-0003): degrade-safe — add finite() guard returning '—', keep valid output byte-identical. Key cross-repo nuance: the ai-bookkeeper ledger formatMoney is fail-loud (throws on non-finite) and that is correct there because it sits behind a validated arithmetic boundary; for a LEAF DISPLAY helper consuming AI-sourced scores with no validation boundary, degrade-safe (this repo's ADR-0002 precedent) is correct, because a throw crashes the render. Future-work (not bundled): route case-file inline ${likelihood}% renders through formatPercent.

Gates on your diff only.\\n\\n## Out of scope (future work — logged, NOT bundled)\\nRoute the case-file inline percent renders (`CaseDetailView.tsx:123,128`, `CaseFileDashboard.tsx:140`, `CaseList.tsx:256`) through `formatPercent` so the guard protects the rendered surface — separate ticket.\\n\\n**Artifacts:** ADR-0003 written to repo decision log; decision note `Decision: format-helpers-non-finite-guard` written to the shared Obsidian vault with wikilinks to prior format/rounding decisions. title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-02 07:54 UTC
format.ts non-finite guard shipped as PR #7 (degrade-safe, ADR-0003) — immigration-paperwork
content: Implemented the Architect's T1 handoff in immigration-paperwork (xkazm04/xprize-immigration-paperwork): all four src/lib/format.ts helpers (formatCurrency, formatSignedCurrency, formatNumber, formatPercent) now guard with finite(value)=typeof number && Number.isFinite and return placeholder '—' for non-finite input; valid output byte-identical. Fixes formatPercent throwing TypeError on null/undefined via .toFixed (the live crash) and $NaN/NaN% emission. Regression test src/lib/format.test.ts uses node:test + node:assert/strict (repo convention: npm test = tsx --test 'src/**/*.test.ts'); suite went 122→129 tests. Branch dev-clone/format-helpers-non-finite-guard → PR #7 → main. Key reusable nuance: degrade-safe (return placeholder) is correct for LEAF display helpers consuming AI-sourced values with no validation boundary; fail-loud (throw) is only correct behind a validated arithmetic boundary (e.g. ai-bookkeeper ledger formatMoney) — reconcile, don't blindly port across repos.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-02 07:57 UTC
format.ts non-finite guard (PR #7, T1) reviewed → APPROVE — immigration-paperwork
content: Reviewed PR #7 (commit 9edac58, branch dev-clone/format-helpers-non-finite-guard) hardening src/lib/format.ts. Verdict APPROVE: 0 critical/security/correctness, 1 Low note. The load-bearing correctness contract for these four leaf formatters (formatCurrency, formatSignedCurrency, formatNumber, formatPercent): each MUST keep its `if (!finite(value)) return INVALID` as the FIRST statement so valid-input output stays byte-identical; finite() = typeof number && Number.isFinite (traps null/undefined/NaN/±Infinity/non-number). The actual live defect fixed was formatPercent(null/undefined) calling .toFixed → TypeError crashing the render — pinned by assert.doesNotThrow. format.ts currently has ZERO importers, so the string placeholder '—' return cannot break a caller's number contract; RE-CHECK this when the case-file inline `${likelihood}%` renders get routed through formatPercent (ADR-0003 out-of-scope item) — at that point the placeholder reaches a real surface. Reviewer policy held: re-ran targeted (7/7), full suite (129/129), tsc clean, eslint-on-diff clean independently before approving. Low note for next change: tests cover non-finite numbers but not non-number runtime types.

Only one **Low** note (does not gate): tests exercise non-finite numbers but not non-number runtime types (string/object), which the `unknown` guard already covers — worth a follow-up assertion, not a blocker.

**Verdict: APPROVE.**

title: PR #7 merged — format helpers non-finite guard (T1/ADR-0003)
content: QA-tested and merged Dev Clone PR #7 into main (sha 0bc6a69). format.ts formatters (formatCurrency/formatSignedCurrency/formatNumber/formatPercent) now guard non-finite/non-number input, returning the '—' placeholder per ADR 0003. Suite is 129/129. This is the established convention for these leaf display formatters — future format-helper work should preserve the placeholder-degradation contract rather than throwing.

Security re-scan 2026-06-02 — both HIGH findings STILL OPEN; deps stable
content: Full 3-phase scan of immigration-paperwork (ai-paralegal). Both load-bearing HIGH risks re-verified UNCHANGED and still OPEN (no remediation applied since prior scan): (1) AUTH FAIL-OPEN roles.ts:25 isAttorney() returns true for all signed-in users when ATTORNEY_EMAILS empty → gates getCaseAnyOwner (pglite-store.ts:410 / firestore-store.ts:277) which has NO owner filter (where id=$1 only) → cross-tenant case/PII IDOR via /api/rfe:55 and /api/evidence/categorize:98. Fix: fail CLOSED in prod. (2) PII EGRESS lighttrack.ts:208 enabled=cfg.enabled??true (on by default); client.ts sends full {input:prompt,output:text} to LIGHTTRACK_URL every AI call → prompts carry immigration PII = exfil if URL remote. Fix: default capture off in prod + redact. Dependency baseline HELD: 0 critical/0 high, 10 moderate; root causes postcss<8.5.10 XSS (GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93 via next) + uuid bounds-check advisory (via gaxios/teeny-request→firebase-admin/@google-cloud). Release v0.3.1 cut — format non-finite guard (PATCH, ADR-0003)
content: Cut v0.3.1 from v0.3.0 on 2026-06-02 (repo xkazm04/xprize-immigration-paperwork, pkg immigration-concierge). PATCH bump for PR #7 bug fix: format helpers guard non-finite/non-number input → '—' placeholder (ADR 0003). Confirms the pre-1.0 semver rule: fixes=PATCH. PROCESS LESSON: `main` is branch-protected requiring 2 status checks (Typecheck·Lint·Test·Build + E2E Playwright) — direct push to main is REJECTED, so the release commit (version bump + changelog) must itself go through a release PR (release/vX.Y.Z → main) that passes CI, then merge. Use merge-method 'merge' (NOT squash) so the pre-pushed annotated tag's commit stays reachable from main. Repo-level GitHub native auto-merge is DISABLED, so merge via PUT /pulls/{n}/merge once checks are green. Suite is now 129/129.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-02 08:12 UTC
v0.3.1 docs verified current; Release Manager pre-writes changelog entries
content: For the SDLC2-Immigration repo, the Release Manager persona writes the CHANGELOG entry as part of its version-bump role. So on a `release.version.bumped` event, the changelog is typically ALREADY current by the time Docs Steward runs — my job becomes a currency-verification + README-scoping check, not duplicate changelog authoring. v0.3.1 (format-helper non-finite guard, ADR 0003) was already fully documented in CHANGELOG; README needed no edit because the README's Documentation section links docs/adr/ at the directory level (not per-ADR) and no section references format helpers. Last-processed commit: f3cdab4 (v0.3.1). Rule of thumb: internal leaf display-formatter fixes with byte-identical valid-input output are changelog-worthy but not README-worthy.
